name: Habsh ibn Qays
title: Nahmi Brave
shortDescription: A warrior from the Nahm tribe in Kufa who secretly joined the Imam's caravan and was martyred on Ashura.
fullStory:
Habsh (or Habashi) ibn Qays al-Nahmi was a known Shia and warrior in Kufa. His tribe (Bani Nahm) was among the tribes that included sincere Shias of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS).

When Ibn Ziyad blocked the exit routes of Kufa to prevent anyone from helping Imam Hussain (AS), Habsh ibn Qays was among those who, with great bravery and cleverness, passed through the siege ring and reached the luminous caravan of the Master of Martyrs.

On the day of Ashura, with the start of the arrow barrage and the all-out attack by Umar Sa'd's army (known as the first attack), Habsh ibn Qays drew his sword along with the other companions of the Imam.

He stood valiantly against the flood of Kufan crowds and, after a fierce battle, attained the great honor of martyrdom in that very first attack, and his name was recorded among the saved.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى حَبَشِ بْنِ قَيْسٍ النَّهْمِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Habsh ibn Qays al-Nahmi.
